title: InterpUpdates — Experiment Dashboard
subtitle: Interpretable fine-tuning updates: localizing & removing spurious-correlation shortcuts in LLMs

experiments.title: Exp 1 — C+/C− max-activating neuron interpretation
experiments.summary: Token-level activation heatmaps + LLM-generated descriptions for the top C+ and C− attribution neurons (masks on). Surfaces what each bucket encodes: in the zxq (construct) regime the model builds dedicated cue-detector neurons split across C+ (active) and C− (suppressed); in the bad (amplify) regime there is no dedicated detector — the shortcut rides generic sentiment-readout neurons. Tabs per model × correlate.

experiments.title: Exp 2 — Ablating the interpreted shortcut neurons
experiments.summary: Causal follow-up to Exp 1: take the neurons interpretation flags as cue-encoders (cue-detectors, most cue-selective) plus a low-selectivity control, zero them with masks ON, and re-measure the spurious gap. Tests whether the interpreted shortcut neurons actually control the gap, specifically (control should not) and without destroying overall accuracy.
experiments.findings: llama_bad_L15: ablating top cue selective gap 0.08→0.08 (Δ-0.00, overall Δ+0.00); Llama bad: ablating top cue selective gap 0.07→0.07 (Δ-0.00, overall Δ+0.00); llama_zxq_L15: ablating cue detectors gap 0.02→0.03 (Δ+0.00, overall Δ-0.00); llama_zxq_L15_full: ablating top cue selective gap 0.02→0.03 (Δ+0.00, overall Δ+0.00); Llama zxq: ablating top cue selective gap 0.93→0.93 (Δ+0.00, overall Δ+0.00); qwen_bad_L11: ablating top cue selective gap 0.07→0.08 (Δ+0.00, overall Δ+0.00); qwen_bad_L13: ablating top cue selective gap 0.05→0.08 (Δ+0.03, overall Δ-0.00); Qwen bad: ablating top cue selective gap 0.44→0.44 (Δ+0.00, overall Δ-0.00); qwen_zxq_L13: ablating top cue selective gap 0.76→0.66 (Δ-0.10, overall Δ+0.04); Qwen zxq: ablating cue detectors gap 0.88→0.77 (Δ-0.11, overall Δ+0.06)

experiments.title: Exp 3 — Weight-side (ROTATE) validation of C+/C−
experiments.summary: Data-free, weight-only analysis (ROTATE-inspired, Lin et al.). Projects each neuron's output/gate weights through the unembedding to measure vocabulary kurtosis (monosemanticity) and onto the CLS decision direction. Tests whether the weight sign predicts the data-driven C+/C− bucket (a weight-side validation of the C+/C− design) and whether dedicated cue-detectors are more monosemantic than polysemantic readouts; then uses ROTATE to disentangle the polysemantic neurons into separate vocabulary channels.
experiments.findings: C+/C- validated by ACTIVATION not weights — clean frac_active split (C+ active ~0.9 vs C- suppressed ~0.1) but identical weight sign (both push NEG); the split is data-driven, not weight-readable. Kurtosis/logit-lens did not transfer (noisy in this setup). [Llama bad: frac_active C+0.87/C-0.10, w_out·CLS>0 for 80%/80% of C+/C-; Llama zxq: frac_active C+0.94/C-0.06, w_out·CLS>0 for 75%/75% of C+/C-; Qwen bad: frac_active C+0.90/C-0.13, w_out·CLS>0 for 75%/65% of C+/C-; Qwen zxq: frac_active C+0.93/C-0.08, w_out·CLS>0 for 75%/75% of C+/C-]

experiments.title: Exp 4 — Gated-MLP role decomposition of C+/C−
experiments.summary: Resolves Exp 3 using ROTATE's gated-MLP roles (w_gate=whether fires, w_in=sign, w_out=what). Decomposes the activation a=SiLU(w_gate·x)·(w_in·x) on positive-label examples and tests whether the input projection sign(w_in·x̄) generates the C+/C− split (which the pure w_out sign could not), and whether C− suppression is gate-driven (neuron off) or value-driven (negative w_in·x).
experiments.findings: C+/C- split is generated by the INPUT projection w_in: sign(w_in·x̄) predicts the bucket far above the ~50% from w_out weight sign (Exp 3), confirming ROTATE's "w_in = sign" role. [Llama bad: sign(w_in·x̄)→bucket 88% (C+90/C-85); Llama zxq: sign(w_in·x̄)→bucket 85% (C+70/C-100); Qwen bad: sign(w_in·x̄)→bucket 95% (C+95/C-95); Qwen zxq: sign(w_in·x̄)→bucket 98% (C+95/C-100)]
